sources.name: DESNZ
sources.dataset: Low carbon and renewable energy economy (LCREE) survey

sources.name: ONS
sources.dataset: UK Environmental Goods and Services Sector (EGSS)

metadata.methodology: Low-carbon economy employment is measured in the LCREE survey covering: renewable energy generation, energy efficiency products and services, low-carbon heat, carbon capture, environmental consulting, and related supply chains. The 763,000 figure is for 2023 from DESNZ LCREE. The 12% in deprived communities figure is derived from analysis matching LCREE establishment locations to IMD deciles. Green sector wage premium of +8% is from ONS ASHE analysis of LCREE sector SIC codes vs comparable non-green roles.
knownIssues: LCREE survey methodology was revised in 2019, creating a break in the time series — figures before and after 2019 are not directly comparable.
Definition of 'green job' varies significantly between studies — some include all jobs at companies in green sectors, others only the green-relevant proportion of each worker's role.
Regional distribution data is based on establishment location which may undercount home-based and remote green workers, who may live in different regions than their employer's registered address.
